initial_test_output:
=== Timing Attack Resistance Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Basic auth test ---
Authentication successful
OK: correct password accepted
Authentication failed
OK: wrong password rejected
--- Timing side-channel analysis ---
Running security analysis on compareHashes...
  Static analysis: uses == operator instead of constant-time comparison
VULNERABLE: compareHashes does NOT use constant-time comparison
This enables a timing side-channel attack on the password hash
  Timing ratio (full_match / no_match): 0.860
FAIL: Security test failed
FAIL: timing analysis detected side-channel vulnerability
FAIL: Authentication comparison leaks timing information

FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /build
COPY app/ .

RUN go build -o authserver .

FROM alpine:3.20

RUN apk add --no-cache bash coreutils git && \
    git config --global user.email "bench@needle-bench.cc" && \
    git config --global user.name "needle-bench"

COPY --from=builder /build/authserver /usr/local/bin/authserver

FROM timing-attack-comparison:latest
TOOL shell
TOOL file:read
TOOL file:edit
LIMIT turns 60
LIMIT tokens 400000
LIMIT wall_clock 1200

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"timing-attack/auth"
	"timing-attack/server"
)

func main() {
	if len(os.Args) < 2 {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Usage: authserver <command> [args...]")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr)
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Commands:")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  serve             Start the auth server (blocks)")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  verify <password>  Verify a password against stored hash")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  test              Run security tests")
		os.Exit

--- a/app/auth/compare.go
+++ b/app/auth/compare.go
@@ -1,8 +1,10 @@
 package auth

-// compareHashes compares two hex-encoded hash strings for equality.
+import "crypto/subtle"
+
+// compareHashes compares two hex-encoded hash strings in constant time.
+// Uses crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare to prevent timing side-channel attacks.
 func compareHashes(stored, input string) bool {
 	if len(stored) != len(input) {
 		return false
 	}
-	return stored == input
+	return subtle.ConstantTimeCompare(

# timing-attack-comparison

## Project

A Go authentication server (`authserver`) that stores user credentials as SHA-256 hashes and verifies passwords against the stored hashes. The server exposes an HTTP login endpoint and a CLI for testing. User credentials are stored in an in-memory store with thread-safe access.

## Symptoms

Basic authentication works correctly: valid passwords are accepted, invalid passwords are rejected. However, the security test reveals that the time taken to reject a

[core]
	repositoryformatversion = 0
	filemode = true
	bare = false
	logallrefupdates = true

git config --global user.email "bench@needle-bench.cc" && \
    git config --global user.name "needle-bench"

FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /build
COPY app/ .

RUN go build -o authserver .

FROM alpine:3.20

RUN apk add --no-cache bash coreutils git && \
    git config --global user.email "bench@needle-bench.cc" && \
    git config --global user.name "needle-bench"

COPY --from=builder /build/authserver /usr/local/bin/authserver

WORKDIR /app

COPY . .
RUN chmod +x test.sh

# Install Go toolchain in final image so agent can rebuild
RUN apk add --no-cache go && \
    rm -rf /var/cache/apk/*
